claim: Knowledge graphs complement vector databases in RAG by storing entity relationships, which vector databases do poorly; agents benefit from layering both approaches
actionable: For relational queries (e.g., how do two companies work together), use knowledge graphs; for single-entity queries, use vector search; implement both in your RAG pipeline
source_title: Knowledge Graphs in n8n are FINALLY Here!

claim: Knowledge graphs extract entities and relationships using LLMs (e.g., OpenAI), which is slower and more expensive than vector database operations
actionable: Be aware that knowledge graphs incur per-document LLM costs; don't use them if your data isn't highly relational or if latency/cost is critical
source_title: Knowledge Graphs in n8n are FINALLY Here!

claim: Use an MCP server pattern with knowledge graph libraries (e.g., Graphiti with Neo4j) to enable agents to build and query knowledge graphs in workflows
actionable: Integrate Graphiti MCP server with n8n or similar workflow platforms to automatically extract entities/relationships and expose graph search as an agent tool
source_title: Knowledge Graphs in n8n are FINALLY Here!

claim: Graphiti and Neo4j must run internally (Docker/self-hosted) in n8n; cloud-hosted n8n cannot run MCP servers due to connectivity constraints
actionable: If you want knowledge graphs in n8n, self-host n8n and run Graphiti/Neo4j in Docker containers alongside it; cloud n8n won't work
source_title: Knowledge Graphs in n8n are FINALLY Here!

claim: Agentic RAG means giving agents multiple search strategies (vector, knowledge graph, etc.) and letting them choose which is optimal for each query
actionable: Design your RAG system prompt to guide agents on when to use knowledge graph queries (relational) vs vector search (semantic), and test both approaches
source_title: Knowledge Graphs in n8n are FINALLY Here!
